Incident - Please investigate why the CVS calls for Braftovi are coming in as unmapped. Mapping table has been updated. Codes are BRCRC2, BRCRC3, BRMEL2, BRMEL3, BRNSCLC2, BRNSCLC3

Proceeding with the closure of the incident ticket.

Oncology Historical Files:

•	Pfizer_PSSP_90088_Association

From: Gundanna, Punya (Contractor)
Sent: Tuesday, March 31, 2026 10:12 AM
To: Karlapudi, Harshitha (Contractor) <Harshitha.Karlapudi@pfizer.com>; DL-WBB-BIASM-SALES <DL-WBB-BIASM-SALES@pfizer.com>
Cc: DL-PatientInsightsSupport <DL-PatientInsightsSupport@pfizer.com>
Subject: RE: Patient Insights | RITM0280133 | Inquiry Regarding Data Lag in PUB_ONC_SPP_FCT Table

Hi Harshitha,

Please find below our findings regarding the data availability for the PUB_ONC_SPP_FCT table –

Data Load Process :
The table follows an INSERT OVERWRITE (full refresh) load pattern, where the entire dataset is refreshed during each load cycle. As a result, previous data states are not retained within the table.

Analysis Performed:
Due to the overwrite nature of the table, historical comparisons are limited. We leveraged snowflake time travel to analyze table states at specific timestamps; however, this capability allows analysis only for the past one week. Accordingly, our analysis was performed on the most recent available timeframe.

Key Observations:
1. Using Time Travel analysis, we confirmed that Tuesday(March 24th ) shipment became available on Thursday(March 26) morning, indicating a delay of approximately two days from the shipment date.
2. For March 17th , although data was reported as available, we are unable to verify the exact timing or conditions due to the 7-day time travel retention limitation.
3. All loads are completing successfully within expected timelines, confirming that the ingestion process is stable.
4. There are no issues with the quality or completeness of the data received; the behavior is related to data availability timing.

Conclusion:
With the current one-week analysis window, we are unable to determine whether this behavior is consistent or an exception. We recommend monitoring the upcoming Tuesday runs(next 2-3 cycles) to better understand and validate

INC02898912 - Patient Insight | Job failure - TF_CHAMP_SPIF_KPI_master | PROD

The above two taskflows failed with the below error –
[ERROR] Either the Amazon S3 bucket revealpamrasp36794 does not exist or the user does not have permission to access the bucket. Detailed message from Amazon S3 : [User: arn:aws:iam::604526422050:user/SRVAMR-RVLPRW is not authorized to perform: s3:ListBucket on resource: "arn:aws:s3:::revealpamrasp36794" with an explicit deny in an identity-based policy (Service: S3, Status Code: 403, Request ID: 258QZ1PMH0VKC4B6, Extended Request ID: C688a50kqZpBBh5UG7cYTPQOKObYs2gd3O5MKpbOrIqWRzILUcTR9DcbC9SrwTUKPeXHEUxKWho=)]

RCA - We observed certain infrastructure security policy updates made by the AWS Cloud OPS team that were applied to the service account utilized for IICS jobs in Production, which resulted in the issue. These updates have since been rolled back, and the IICS jobs are now able to connect with S3. We will follow up with Cloud Ops team to understand the reason for this change, and to ensure that any future changes to the access policy doesn’t impact our interfaces.

The CHAMP dashboard has been successfully updated, and the latest Vyndamax data has been loaded into our systems as expected.

Incident : INC02898762 - Patient Insight | Job failure - TF_MASTER_BRDG_FILE_LOAD | PROD

The above two taskflows failed with the below error –
[ERROR] Either the Amazon S3 bucket revealpamrasp36794 does not exist or the user does not have permission to access the bucket. Detailed message from Amazon S3 : [User: arn:aws:iam::604526422050:user/SRVAMR-RVLPRW is not authorized to perform: s3:ListBucket on resource: "arn:aws:s3:::revealpamrasp36794" with an explicit deny in an identity-based policy (Service: S3, Status Code: 403, Request ID: 258QZ1PMH0VKC4B6, Extended Request ID: C688a50kqZpBBh5UG7cYTPQOKObYs2gd3O5MKpbOrIqWRzILUcTR9DcbC9SrwTUKPeXHEUxKWho=)]

RCA - We observed certain infrastructure security policy updates made by the AWS Cloud OPS team that were applied to the service account utilized for IICS jobs in Production, which resulted in the issue. These updates have since been rolled back, and the IICS jobs are now able to connect with S3. We will follow up with Cloud Ops team to understand the reason for this change, and to ensure that any future changes to the access policy doesn’t impact our interfaces.

Resolution - The CHAMP dashboard has been successfully updated, and the latest Vyndamax data has been loaded into our systems as expected.

RESOLUTION:
Problem:
In Enhanced Services, the Incremental Transactions and Calculated Ratio show significant variation with every production load.
RCA:
During patient-pair generation, the Patient ID changes across loads, resulting in a different or additional set of patient pairs being produced each time. This leads to changes in the Inc_Avg values for the corresponding brands. Since Incremental Revenue—a key input for Ratio calculation—depends on these values, the Ratio fluctuates across loads.
Resolution:
To ensure consistent results, we plan to enhance the patient-pair matching mechanism so that the same Patient IDs are generated reliably across all runs. A Jira ticket https://digitalpfizer.atlassian.net/browse/PSSPI-9074 has been created for this enhancement and handed over to the L3 team for further action.
BRIEF_DESCRIPTION: Patient Insights | Enhancement | Enhanced Services-Financial-Incremental Trx Difference - JIRA -
